name: Pike Push-Up
cues: Hips high, body in an inverted V
Lower the crown of the head toward the floor
execution_steps: Start in a push-up position, then walk your feet in and raise your hips into an inverted-V pike.
Set your hands slightly wider than your shoulders, keep your legs straight, and stack your shoulders over your hands.
Bend your elbows to lower the crown of your head toward the floor between your hands.
Press back up to full arm extension, keeping your hips high throughout.
good_when: You are training shoulders at home or with no equipment.
You are building toward a handstand push-up.
You want a bodyweight vertical press for the front delts.
bad_when: You need to add external load to keep progressing.
Wrist or shoulder mobility makes the inverted position painful.
You want precise, isolated front-delt loading (a machine or dumbbell press is easier to load).
